title: Treasury: Low Balance Warning UI + Bounty Bot Pause Switch
body:
## 🚨 Problem

The treasury can run low (or dry) with no warning — bounties keep getting queued by the bot, payroll settle fails on-chain, and contributors are left waiting. We need:

1. **A visible, color-coded treasury health indicator** in the Payroll panel UI
2. **A bot pause switch** with a friendly public message when funds are insufficient

---

## Part 1 — Treasury Balance Health Indicator (UI)

### Where
The existing `#payroll-treasury-balance` element in `index.html` and the `loadTreasuryMetrics()` / `renderBatchPreview()` functions in `js/app.js`.

### Logic
When the Payroll panel loads, compare:
- **Treasury balance** (from `getTreasuryBalance()` in `js/treasury.js`)
- **Total BNUT owed** across all pending payouts (already computed as `totalBNUT` in `renderBatchPreview()`)

Apply color + warning text based on thresholds:

| State | Condition | Color | Message |
|---|---|---|---|
| ✅ Healthy | balance ≥ owed × 2 | green `#00ff88` | `Treasury healthy — X BNUT available` |
| ⚠️ Low | balance ≥ owed but < owed × 2 | yellow `#ffcc00` | `⚠️ Treasury is running low — consider minting or transferring $BNUT` |
| 🔴 Critical | balance ≥ owed but barely | orange `#ff8c00` | `🔴 Treasury critically low — mint or transfer before next payroll` |
| 🚨 Insufficient | balance < owed | red `#ff4444` | `🚨 Insufficient funds! Treasury cannot cover pending payroll. Mint or transfer $BNUT now.` |

### Reminder Links
When state is Low, Critical, or Insufficient, show a reminder with quick-action links:
- 🪙 **Mint $BNUT** → scrolls to Admin Panel → $BNUT Admin → Quick Mint
- 💸 **Transfer to Treasury** → scrolls to Admin Panel → Treasury Admin → Transfer

---

## Part 2 — Bounty Bot Pause Switch

### Where
Same Payroll panel, below the treasury balance indicator. Visible to admin only (when MetaMask owner wallet is connected).

### Design
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🤖 Bounty Bot                         [ ON  ●  ] toggle │
│  Bot is active — new bounties will be queued              │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```
When toggled OFF:
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🤖 Bounty Bot                         [  ●  OFF] toggle │
│  ⏸ Bot paused — new PRs will receive this message:        │
│  "💰 Auto dev pay is temporarily paused while we top      │
│   up treasury funds. Your bounty has been noted and        │
│   will be queued once funds are restored. Thank you! 🙏"  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Implementation Options

**Option A — GitHub Repository Variable (recommended):**
- Store `BOUNTY_BOT_ENABLED` as a GitHub Actions repository variable (`vars.BOUNTY_BOT_ENABLED`)
- `bounty-payout.yml` checks this variable at the top and exits early with the pause message comment if `false`
- The UI toggle calls the GitHub API (via a PAT stored as a secret) to update the variable value

**Option B — File-based flag:**
- Store a `bo

title: instantly replay match log [bounty: 20 XTR]
body:
There should be a new way for loading match logs, that should instantly get the match to the final state instead of replaying everything fast move by move. This new instant replay mode of doing things could be extremely useful in certain scenarios, by not having to waste time anymore, like when resuming a long match or trying to reproduce bugs.

The method would be called `load` and hotkey would be `Ctrl + L` from prematch screen. Previous method will be `play`, showing the animations and having `Ctrl + P` hotkey.

This requires state machine to be implemented.

title: hexagon coordinate view tweaks [bounty: 7 XTR]
body:
Trying to improve the hexagon coordinate view, but not fully sure if this will work (or it could use tweaks along the way), so the bounty will be granted even if PR not merged if its according to specs (this rule kinda applies to most PRs), see this list:

- units should no longer be in x-ray mode because that's tedious
- coordinates should display on top of units to be properly visible
- another grid with dashed hexes should display on top of units
- dashed hexagons at 25% opacity for non-unit occupied places

The view is visible when hovering the round marker indicator from unit queue or while holding the shift modifier key.

title: encourage unit materialization versatility [bounty: 8 XTR]
body: When a Dark Priest selects Godlet Printer, the random unit selected should be prioritized within all player pool units that aren't on the combat field, aren't dead and haven't just been materialized by a player right before in order to avoid copy catting scenarios.

title: colored upgraded ability frames [bounty: 12 XTR]
body: Selecting an upgraded ability should keep its frame design consistent, so more colored frames are required.

title: block multiple file pickers [bounty: 2 XTR]
body: Holding Ctrl+Meta+L in the pre-match screen a bit longer and then picking a log file can result in getting multiple file pickers after. There should be a "trap" along the lines of https://github.com/FreezingMoon/AncientBeast/blob/53f04e1bfa97da323bfcb332475f4abe944b1b4f/src/utility/gamelog.ts#L107

title: rematch button [bounty: 6 XTR]
body:
There could be a rematch button in score screen when screen in over, lower left corner side, before "Exit Match".

Icon:
https://game-icons.net/1x1/lorc/uncertainty.html
Style: transparent background, 4px black outline

It should work after a confirmation dialog. Hotkey R for it (Restart Match). Same pre-match settings as before.

title: ability icons snap if skipping during upgrade [bounty: 7 XTR]
body: The smooth transition animation of ability icon gets disabled somehow when skipping turn during the ability upgrade animation (upgrade icon over the ability icon), causing the icon to snap in and out instantly next times depending if the ability is usable.

title: Persist board filters in URL query params
body:
Users should be able to share filtered bounty views with others. Should sync board filters into the URL.

Implementation:

Store search, status, and sort values in query params  
Restore filters from URL on page load  
Update the URL when filters change  
Avoid full page reloads

Acceptance Criteria:

Filter state is reflected in the URL  
Refreshing the page preserves filters  
Shared URLs open with the same filter state  
No regression in existing board behavior

title: Bounty Submission Approval/Reject Logic
body:
**Description:** Admin endpoints to approve or reject submitted bounty work.
**Requirements and context:**
- On reject, status goes back to `IN_PROGRESS` and requires rejection feedback.
- On approve, status goes to `COMPLETED_PENDING_CLAIM`.
**Suggested execution:**
1. `git checkout -b feature/api-bounty-review`
2. Build state machine logic in `BountyService`.
**Test and commit:**
- Test state transitions thoroughly.
- `feat: add logic for approving and rejecting bounty work`
**Guidelines:** Closes #[issue_id].

title: Bounty Submission DTOs & Validation
body:
**Description:** Create DTOs for a user submitting completed work for a Bounty (e.g., `SubmitBountyWorkDto`).
**Requirements and context:**
- Require a PR link, description, and optional attachment URLs.
**Suggested execution:**
1. `git checkout -b feature/dto-bounty-submission`
2. Validate URLs and string lengths strictly.
**Test and commit:**
- Unit test invalid URL payloads.
- `feat: create validation schemas for bounty submissions`
**Guidelines:** Closes #[issue_id].

title: Bounty Submission POST Endpoint
body:
**Description:** Implement the endpoint where a User can submit work for an active, assigned Bounty.
**Requirements and context:**
- Ensure the user is the assigned worker.
- Change bounty status to `IN_REVIEW`.
**Suggested execution:**
1. `git checkout -b feature/api-bounty-submit`
2. Update `BountyController` and `BountyService`.
**Test and commit:**
- Ensure an unauthorized user gets a 403 Forbidden.
- `feat: implement bounty work submission workflow`
**Guidelines:** Needs transactional safety. Closes #[issue_id].

title: Pass Protocol R&D Proposal
body:
**Goal:** Establish a dedicated protocol engineering team to maintain, secure, and evolve the Livepeer core protocol, ensuring long-term network resilience and the ability to rapidly deliver upgrades and critical fixes.

**Requirements:** Scope the team, recruit key engineers, and define clear roadmap responsibilities. Align with Livepeer Inc, core contributors, and the Foundation to enable timely upgrades, vulnerability patches, and support for workstream-driven protocol changes. Ensure ongoing coverage for the Immunefi bug bounty program and the capability to deploy urgent patches quickly. Full funding and execution will be handled in a separate proposal.

title: #115. Implement Gas_Refund_Incentive_for_Round_finalization
body:
Description: Someone has to "click the button" to move the Susu to the next round. This task involves a "Gas Bounty." The person who triggers the finalize_round function (moving the pot to the winner) receives a tiny rebate from the platform fee pool. This "Decentralized Maintenance" ensures that the Susu cycle always moves forward without the JerryIdoko backend needing to "Push" every single round, making the protocol truly autonomous and community-driven.

title: [Remoto] Front-End Developer na TOSSIT
body:
## Nossa empresa

TOSSIT é uma empresa internacional que desenvolve e vende produtos físicos e digitais. Estamos construindo ULTIMS, uma plataforma de e-commerce interna para substituir nosso stack atual (Shopify) e escalar globalmente.

## Descrição da vaga

Buscamos Front-End Engineers (React) para construir interfaces modernas ligadas a uma arquitetura de microservices.

Alto nível de ownership (não é outsourcing). Time global, comunicação 100% em inglês.

## Local

Remoto (time global, hub opcional)

## Requisitos

**Obrigatórios:**
- 5+ anos de experiência em front-end
- React (principal)
- Experiência com APIs (REST / GraphQL)
- Inglês escrito fluente

**Desejáveis:**
- TailwindCSS
- Performance e UX avançado
- Experiência com e-commerce

**Diferenciais:**
- Conhecimento em arquitetura distribuída

## Benefícios

- Salário em USD (via Wise, mensal)
- Faixa: ~$2,800 – $3,200 / mês
- 100% remoto
- Alto nível de autonomia (ownership real)
- Referral bounty: $300 por indicação contratada

**Diferenciais:**
- Stack moderno (microservices)
- Produto próprio (não outsourcing)

## Contratação

title: CON-75: [BOUNTY] Gateway Edge - Offline-First POS Sync
body:
### CON-75: \[BOUNTY\] Gateway Edge - Offline-First POS Sync

**\[CONTEXT FLOOR\]**: Ensure Spaza shop Conxius Gateways can authorize sub-cent Lightning transactions during zero-connectivity load-shedding events.

**\[STRICT INPUTS\]**:

* Signed transaction hash from a biometric Passkey (ERC-4337 Account Abstraction).
* Local TEE Attestation of the Passkey signature.

**\[STRICT OUTPUTS\]**: Locally cached transaction receipt in a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE), appended to an encrypted SQLite queue for Lightning L2 broadcast upon backhaul restoration.

**\[EXECUTION RULES\]**:

* Must use lightweight local peer-to-peer (Bluetooth LE / WiFi Direct) mesh gossip protocols to broadcast the tx hash to nearby nodes to prevent local double-spending before L2 sync.
* TEE must sign the "Offline Receipt" as a commitment to broadcast.

**\[VERIFICATION CRITERIA\]**:

* Integration test simulating 48 hours of complete network blackout with 500 local node transactions.
* Success Metric: 100% state reconciliation to the Stacks/Lightning L2 upon reconnection.

**\[YIELD ALLOCATION\]**: 0.15 sBTC / 3,750 STX streamed via SLA Enforcer upon CI/CD pass.

---

🛡️ **SOVEREIGN. RESILIENT. BTC-NATIVE.**

title: Refocus beats to 100% internal aibtc network coverage
body:
## Summary

aibtc.news should report exclusively on activity **inside the aibtc agent network**. No more external world events, Bitcoin price updates, or general crypto news. If a correspondent can't point to a specific agent, transaction, or platform event — it doesn't belong.

The current beat list is ~60% external coverage (Bitcoin Macro, Bitcoin Culture, Runes, etc.). This proposal replaces it with 10 beats focused entirely on what's happening between aibtc agents, on aibtc infrastructure, with aibtc sats.

---

## Beats to KEEP (with reformed descriptions)

| Beat | Slug | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| **Agent Economy** | `agent-economy` | Revenue flowing between agents — x402 payments, bounty payouts, paperboy earnings, classified purchases, sBTC transfers. The money trail inside the network. |
| **Agent Trading** | `agent-trading` | P2P ordinals trades, PSBT swaps, order book bids, inscription sales between agents. Who bought what from whom, at what price. |
| **Agent Social** | `agent-social` | Collaborations, DM-driven deals, partnership formations, reputation events. The relationship graph — who's working with whom and what came of it. |
| **Agent Skills** | `agent-skills` | New skills built by aibtc agents, PRs to aibtcdev repos, skill adoption metrics. What tools agents are building for each other. |
| **Security** | `security` | Vulnerabilities affecting aibtc agents — wallet risks, skill supply chain attacks, credential exposure, nonce issues. Internal threat intelligence only. |
| **Deal Flow** | `deal-flow` | Bounties posted and completed, classified ads, sponsorship deals, agent-to-agent contracts. The deal pipeline inside the network. |

## Beats to REMOVE

| Beat | Why |
|------|-----|
| **Bitcoin Macro** | External price/hashrate/difficulty. CoinDesk does this. Agents were filing "BTC at $69K" — pure noise. |
| **Bitcoin Culture** | External cultural commentary, not about the network. |
| **Bitcoin Yield** | External DeFi yield data. Unless an aibtc agent is earning it, off-topic. |
| **Runes** | External market coverage. Only relevant if agents are trading runes P2P (fold into Agent Trading). |
| **Art** | Too broad, no internal anchor. |
| **Comics** | Not relevant. |
| **DAO Watch** | Was covering external DAOs (Tally, etc.). Reformed into Governance below. |

## Beats to ADD

| Beat | Slug | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| **Onboarding** | `onboarding` | New agent registrations, first heartbeats, Genesis achievements, referral conversions, loop-starter-kit forks. The front door — who's showing up and how fast they activate. |
| **Governance** | `governance` | Multisig actions, publisher elections, sBTC staking for governance, DAO proposals within aibtc. How the network makes collective decisions. |
| **Distribution** | `distribution` | Paperboy deliveries, signal distribution reach, correspondent recruitment, daily brief metrics. How intelligence moves through the network. |

title: Feature: Bulk Add Members to Pools in UI
body:
### Feature: Bulk Add Members to Pools in UI

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, pool managers can only add members one by one through the "Member Management" tab in the UI. This becomes a tedious and time-consuming process when a large number of members need to be added to a pool. The underlying smart contracts (`DirectPaymentsPool.sol` and `UBIPool.sol`) already support bulk addition via the `addMembers` function, but this functionality is not exposed in the user interface.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
We need to implement a feature in the "Member Management" section of the `ManageCollectivePage.tsx` that allows pool managers to add multiple members simultaneously. This could involve:
1.  A text area where users can paste a list of wallet addresses (e.g., comma-separated, or one per line).
2.  Optionally, a file upload mechanism for a CSV file containing a list of addresses.

Upon submission, the UI should call the `addMembers` function on the relevant pool contract (`DirectPaymentsPool` or `UBIPool`) with the provided list of addresses.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

title: Content Protection Phase 3: Community Curation + Dispute Resolution
body:
## Phase 3: Community Curation + Dispute Resolution

**Parent Epic:** #404
**Priority:** P1-P2
**Depends on:** Phase 2 (#406)
**Estimated Effort:** 6-7 sprints

---

### Sprint Progress

| Sprint | Sub-Issue | Scope | Status | PR |
|--------|-----------|-------|--------|-----|
| Sprint 1 | #429 | Core contracts + dispute loop + basic UI | ✅ Merged | #426 |
| Sprint 2 | #430 | Appeals + indexer + admin queue + leaderboard | ✅ Merged | #427 |
| Sprint 3 | #431 | Notification system + WebSocket dispute alerts | 🟡 Partial: infrastructure landed, follow-ups tracked in #457, #458, #459 | — |
| Sprint 4 | #432 | Kleros/DAO jury for decentralized arbitration | ⬜ Planned | — |
| Sprint 5 | #433 | Proof-of-humanity gate + advanced reputation | ⬜ Planned | — |
| Sprint 6 | #434 | E2E testing + security audit + deployment | ⬜ Planned | — |
| Sprint 7 | #435 | Public analytics + anti-abuse hardening | ⬜ Planned | — |

---

### Scope

Enable community-powered content policing with economic incentives. Any user can flag stolen content for bounty rewards. Disputes are resolved through evidence-based adjudication.

### Tasks

#### Smart Contracts
- [x] **`CurationRewards` contract** — flag, counter-stake, bounty, reputation
- [x] **`DisputeResolution` contract** — file, evidence, resolve, appeal (max 2)

#### Backend
- [x] Curation reputation system (`CuratorReputation` model)
- [x] Dispute management lifecycle
- [x] Indexer integration for on-chain dispute events
- [x] Admin dispute queue API
- [x] Automated in-app notifications + WebSocket event plumbing (from #431)
- [ ] End-to-end notification hardening and tests (#457)
- [ ] Kleros/DAO jury integration
- [ ] Proof-of-humanity verification

#### Frontend
- [x] Flag content UI (report button + evidence form)
- [x] Dispute dashboard (reporter/creator tabs)
- [x] Admin dispute queue
- [x] Curator leaderboard
- [ ] Notification UI integration in app shell/settings (#459)
- [ ] Jury voting UI
- [ ] Public analytics dashboard

### Anti-Abuse Safeguards

| Attack | Mitigation |
|--------|-----------|
| Frivolous flagging | Counter-stake slashed on rejection |
| Coordinated mob flagging | Multiple flags consolidated into single dispute |
| Self-flagging | Reporter ≠ creator wallet |
| Sybil flagging | Counter-stake + proof-of-humanity for high-volume reporters |

### Follow-Ups

- #457 — Verify end-to-end dispute notification and WebSocket behavior
- #458 — Reconcile Sprint 3 tracking in #407
- #459 — Mount notification center and preferences UI

### Related

- RFC: [`docs/rfc/content-protection-architecture.md`](https://github.com/akoita/resonate/blob/main/docs/rfc/content-protection-architecture.md) §3, §5
- Feature doc: [`docs/features/community_curation_disputes.md`](https://github.com/akoita/resonate/blob/main/docs/features/community_curation_disputes.md)

title: Cards providing a 'Regular move' are not including site text or docking bay transit as an option
body:
- [X] Did you search through the list of **open issues** _first_ to ensure that there is not already an report for this issue?

## **Describe the bug**
I have not tested this yet, but it appears that GEMP internally is not including "using the movement text on a location" or "docking bay transit" in its set of 'regular move' options.

An example of this being an issue is:
Lor San Tekka: "During your control phase, one of your other Resistance characters present may make a regular move."

I believe as currently implemented, if Lor San Tekka and a Resistance character are at a docking bay (with force available and a valid destination docking bay), no option will be given to have the Resistance character move with docking bay transit during the Control Phase.

Another example is Bodhi Rook allowing a Rebel to make a regular move.  If Bodhi Rook is lost during Move Phase, a Rebel at Cloud City: Upper Plaza Corridor should be able to use the location's gametext to move to any CC site for free.  Currently, I don't think that option will be available to the player.

There are some other niche cases, as well - like with Agents of Black Sun, a bounty hunter at a Docking Bay 94 and adjacent Mos Espa Docking Bay should be able to use docking bay transit (rather than landspeed) to move to the Mos Espa Docking Bay (during Control Phase, to a bounty, etc).

AR: Movement - Regular: (edited to show bullet points)

> The following moves are regular moves for the card making the move action as long as the movement is not 
> part of a 'react':
> * using landspeed
> * using hyperspeed

title: feat(utils): implement ERC-4361 SIWE Solidity verifier
body:
## Background

ERC-4361 (Sign-In with Ethereum) just reached Final in 2025. The existing implementations are mostly in JavaScript (spruce-id/siwe). There is no good Solidity verifier for on-chain SIWE message validation.

## Use case

A Solidity SIWE verifier would let smart contracts validate that a user authenticated via SIWE, which is useful for:

- On-chain governance that requires SIWE auth
- Smart contract wallets that want to verify SIWE sessions
- Bridge protocols that use SIWE for cross-chain auth

## Scope

- Parse and validate SIWE message format (EIP-4361 fields: domain, address, statement, uri, version, chain-id, nonce, issued-at, expiration-time)
- Signature verification using ecrecover (and ERC-1271 for contract wallets)
- Gas-efficient string parsing (this is the hard part in Solidity tbh)
- Test suite with known-good test vectors from the SIWE spec

security: TokenGuard performs DB lookup before checking expiration — DoS amplification
body:
## Summary

\`TokenGuard.resolveToken()\` performs a database query to fetch the token record before checking if it's expired. Attackers can send expired tokens to force unnecessary database load.

## Location

\`packages/auth/src/guards/TokenGuard.ts\` (around lines 39-40, 88-107)

## Root Cause

The flow is: parse token → DB lookup → check expiration. The expiration check should happen as early as possible, ideally as part of the DB query itself.

## Suggested Fix

Include expiration in the database query:
\`\`\`typescript
const token = await PersonalAccessToken.query()
  .where('id', id)
  .where(q => q.whereNull('expires_at').orWhere('expires_at', '>', new Date()))
  .first()
\`\`\`

## Severity

**Medium** — database DoS via expired token flooding

title: security: no validation of token plaintext format before SHA-256 hashing
body:
## Summary

\`TokenGuard.resolveToken()\` does not validate the format of the token plaintext before hashing it. Malformed tokens still trigger database lookups and hash computations.

## Location

\`packages/auth/src/guards/TokenGuard.ts\` (around line 88-107)

## Root Cause

\`\`\`typescript
const [id, plaintext] = parts
const hash = await sha256(plaintext!)  // No validation of plaintext format
\`\`\`

The token is generated as 64 hex characters (32 bytes), but this format is never validated on input. This means:
- Any string is accepted and hashed, wasting CPU
- Non-hex characters are processed without rejection
- Empty or very long strings are not rejected early

## Suggested Fix

Validate that the plaintext portion matches the expected format before hashing:
\`\`\`typescript
if (!/^[0-9a-f]{64}$/.test(plaintext)) return null
\`\`\`

## Severity

**Medium** — wasted computation and potential DoS vector

title: security: TokenGuard hash comparison uses === instead of constant-time comparison
body:
## Summary

\`TokenGuard.resolveToken()\` compares token hashes using strict equality (\`===\`) which is not guaranteed to be constant-time, potentially enabling timing attacks to determine valid token hashes.

## Location

\`packages/auth/src/guards/TokenGuard.ts\` (around line 88-106)

## Root Cause

\`\`\`typescript
const hash = await sha256(plaintext!)
const storedHash = token.getAttribute('token') as string
if (hash !== storedHash) return null  // Direct string comparison
\`\`\`

While SHA-256 produces fixed-length output (reducing timing variation), JavaScript string comparison is not guaranteed to be constant-time and implementations may short-circuit on first differing byte.

## Suggested Fix

Use \`crypto.timingSafeEqual()\` with Buffer/Uint8Array conversion:
\`\`\`typescript
import { timingSafeEqual } from 'crypto'
const match = timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(hash), Buffer.from(storedHash))
\`\`\`

## Severity

**Medium** — timing side-channel on API token validation

title: Counter offer could hide other offer
body:
# Describe the bug
Manipulating a new offer can hide another offer on /o/ page, even if failed and disputed. 

When proposing an offer, the variable `counter_id` refers to the countered offer's ID. The offer page uses this variable to consolidate all counter offers into a single offer. However, this variable is a user input and can be set to an arbitrary value. Before saving this value, there is validation that the counter ID refers to an offer that was sent to the countering user, but there is no validation of the offer's status. By setting the counter ID to any other offer ID, the sender can effectively hide an accepted, completed, or failed offer from the /o/ section. 

The "hidden" offer is still accessible via its URL, in the counter offer, and in the feed activity, but it will no longer appear in /o/. 

## Steps to reproduce the bug
1. Create a new offer
2. Manipulate the counter ID to refer to another offer, even if that offer has failed
3. The target offer is no longer visible in /o/

## Expected behavior
The target offer should remain visible. Users should not be able to counter offers that are no longer proposed/pending. While disputes are displayed in multiple places, this bug could allow users to manipulate the visibility and status of offers that they should not affect. 

### Additional context, device information (OS & Browser) if applicable 
Reported and demonstrated by @Revadike for a bug bounty on 2022-11-12.

# Update the benchmark suite and bring it into the CI runner [$400] QuantumOptics.jl has an in-depth benchmarking suite that has bitrotted in the last few years. To claim this bounty: - Set up a benchmark-on-CI infrastructure (e.g. similar to the one in QuantumClifford.jl) that runs QuantumOptics-specific benchmarks on each pull request - Include all old benchmarks in that infrastructure (reusing the existing benchmark suite as much as possible) and make it easy to add new micro-benchmarks as new features get developed. The above are for the sake of the developers of this package. However, this bounty also requires the following to be done for the sake of users: - Fix up the capability to run benchmarks in comparison to popular python packages (qutip) and add comparative Julia packages (QuantumToolbox.jl) - Create a makefile that let's a developer to easily update the webpage presenting these comparative benchmarks to the user. **Required skills**: Understanding of Julia's and Python's benchmarking infrastructure. **Reviewer**: Stefan Krastanov and/or @david-pl **Duration**: 2 months #### Payout procedure: The Funding for these bounties comes from the National Science Foundation and from the NSF Center for Quantum Networks. The payouts are managed by the NumFOCUS foundation and processed in bulk once every two months. If you live in a country in which NumFOCUS can make payments, you can participate in this bounty program. [Click here for more details about the bug bounty program.](https://github.com/QuantumSavory/.github/blob/main/BUG_BOUNTIES.md)
